description: The Bosnian mafia comprises illegal gangs and criminal organizations operating in Bosnia and Herzegovina and its diaspora. Involved in drug trafficking, smuggling, extortion, and corruption, with smuggling revenues estimated between €600 million and €900 million. Groups expanded to South America for cocaine trafficking. The organization faces challenges from the country's fragmented governance.

country_links.evidence_quote: They have local chapters in at least 4 countries: Germany, Austria, Bosnia and Spain.
country_links.context: The United Tribuns has local chapters in Austria.

country_links.evidence_quote: founded in 2004 by Armin Ćulum in Villingen-Schwenningen in Baden-Württemberg ... They have local chapters in at least 4 countries: Germany
country_links.context: The United Tribuns motorcycle club was founded in Germany and has local chapters there.

country_links.evidence_quote: one of their more notorious activities occurred in 2003 when they smuggled and sold 250,000 Zastava M70 Yugoslav-made AK-47s to Iraq.
country_links.context: The Bosnian mafia smuggled and sold 250,000 AK-47s to Iraq in 2003.

country_links.evidence_quote: controlling one-third of the cocaine that enters the port of Rotterdam
country_links.context: The Tito and Dino Cartel controls one-third of the cocaine that enters the port of Rotterdam.

country_links.evidence_quote: It has also a strong presence in Peru , which according to a Peruvian official, the cartel is the main buyer of drugs in the country.
country_links.context: The Tito and Dino Cartel has a strong presence in Peru as the main buyer of drugs in the country.

country_links.evidence_quote: They have local chapters in at least 4 countries: Germany, Austria, Bosnia and Spain.
country_links.context: The United Tribuns has local chapters in Spain.

country_links.evidence_quote: As of 2021 the cartel was based in Dubai
country_links.context: The Tito and Dino Cartel was based in Dubai as of 2021.
